understanding.project_summary:
Confirmed findings:
- AutoDocX is a Python FastAPI application that scans local Python projects, builds structured project context, asks an LLM to generate a structured project understanding, and converts that understanding into standalone HTML documentation. The application exposes a REST API (app.api.project_routes) and includes a Streamlit UI (ui.streamlit_app) that calls the API.
- The project uses storage components to persist scans (ScanStorage), understandings (UnderstandingStorage), and generated HTML documents (DocumentStorage). Scans and understandings are saved as JSON under workspace paths described in code comments (workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json and workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json). Document files are stored under a documents path exposed by app.core.config constants.

Reasonable inferences (uncertain):
- The primary runtime use-case is: scan a project, generate an LLM understanding, and produce HTML documentation; endpoints support individual steps (scan, build context, understand, generate document) and a combined endpoint (/api/projects/documentation/sync) that orchestrates the full workflow. The LLM integration supports multiple providers via a factory (OpenAIProvider, AnthropicProvider) and uses provider, API key, and optional model from app.core.config or request parameters.
understanding.architecture_overview:
Confirmed findings:
- Components:
  - API layer: app.api.project_routes defines HTTP endpoints and orchestrates service calls.
  - Core config: app.core.config supplies runtime configuration (LLM provider selection, API keys, model names) and creates necessary directories.
  - Models: app.models.project defines Pydantic request/response models used across endpoints.
  - Scanner & parser: app.services.project_scanner uses app.services.code_parser.PythonCodeParser and app.services.project_analyzer.ProjectAnalyzer to parse files and derive project-wide statistics and relationships.
  - Context & understanding: app.services.project_context_builder builds LLM-ready context; app.services.project_understanding_service uses app.services.llm.factory.LLMProviderFactory + prompts to call an LLM and produce structured JSON understanding.
  - LLM providers: app.services.llm.openai_provider.OpenAIProvider and app.services.llm.anthropic_provider.AnthropicProvider implement BaseLLMProvider.
  - Comparison: app.services.scan_comparator.ScanComparator compares two stored scans to detect added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes, and dependencies.
  - Storage: app.services.scan_storage.ScanStorage, app.services.understanding_storage.UnderstandingStorage, app.services.document_storage.DocumentStorage persist JSON results and HTML files.
  - Document builder: app.services.document_builder.DocumentBuilder converts stored understanding into HTML.
  - UI: ui.streamlit_app provides helper functions to call and display API results.

- The API router (router) in project_routes is mounted into the FastAPI app in app.main.
- Internal dependencies are explicit: project_routes imports and composes ScanStorage, UnderstandingStorage, ProjectScanner, ProjectUnderstandingService, ScanComparator, DocumentBuilder, DocumentStorage, ProjectContextBuilder, etc.

Reasonable inferences (uncertain):
- The system is synchronous (no async functions reported) and likely intended for single-request orchestration where LLM calls and file IO are performed synchronously. That implies blocking behavior during LLM calls and file reads/writes.

execution_flow.title: Client request to API (entrypoint)
execution_flow.description: A client (UI or CLI) calls a FastAPI endpoint. The Streamlit UI uses sync_documentation to call /api/projects/documentation/sync; other endpoints are available for scanning, understanding, comparison, and document operations.

execution_flow.title: Request validation and orchestration
execution_flow.description: project_routes receives the request, validates using Pydantic models from app.models.project, and begins orchestration for the requested operation (scan, build context, understand, generate, update, or sync).

execution_flow.title: Project scanning and parsing
execution_flow.description: When a scan is required, ProjectScanner.scan_project enumerates the project files, computes hashes and size statistics, and uses PythonCodeParser.parse_file to extract code structure (imports, functions, classes, routes, content hashes). ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project builds project-wide relationships and statistics from parsed files.

execution_flow.title: Persist scan results
execution_flow.description: ScanStorage.save_scan persists the scan JSON under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json. Subsequent read operations request get_scan/get_latest_scan/list_scans as needed.

execution_flow.title: Compare scans (optional)
execution_flow.description: If a previous scan or document exists, ScanComparator.compare_scans compares two stored scans to determine added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes, and dependencies; comparison results can drive update decisions.

execution_flow.title: Build LLM context and generate understanding
execution_flow.description: ProjectContextBuilder.build_context converts a stored scan to either detailed or LLM-optimized context. ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding builds prompts (using build_project_understanding_prompt and constants like PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT), creates an LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory.create_provider (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider), and calls provider.generate_json to obtain a structured JSON understanding. The resulting understanding is saved via UnderstandingStorage.save_understanding.

execution_flow.title: Generate HTML and save document
execution_flow.description: DocumentBuilder.build_html converts the stored understanding into HTML (escaping where needed). DocumentStorage.save_document writes the HTML file and metadata (including links to scan and understanding identifiers); DocumentStorage.get_document_file returns a Path used by FileResponse to serve HTML via get_project_document_html.

module_responsibilities.responsibility: HTTP API routes and orchestration: defines endpoints for scanning, comparing scans, building context, generating understandings, listing and retrieving scans/understandings/documents, and a combined documentation synchronization endpoint that orchestrates scanning, comparing, LLM understanding generation, and document creation.

Application configuration and environment initialization: provides helper functions to create required directories and to read/normalize LLM provider, API key, and model names from environment/configuration. Exposes constants referenced by storage modules (DOCUMENTS_PATH, UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH inferred from usage).
FastAPI application entry point: instantiates the FastAPI app, mounts/uses the router defined in app.api.project_routes, and provides basic health endpoints.

Pydantic request and response models for API validation and serialization: defines ProjectScanRequest, ProjectScanResponse, ProjectContextRequest/Response, ProjectUnderstandingRequest/Response, DocumentGenerationRequest/Response, DocumentUpdateRequest/Response, DocumentationSyncRequest/Response, and summary/storage information models.

Parse Python source files: PythonCodeParser.parse_file reads a .py file and extracts imports, functions, async functions, classes, methods, FastAPI routes, content hashes and syntax errors to produce a structured representation used by the project analyzer.

High-level project scanning: enumerates files, ignores irrelevant paths, computes file hashes and project statistics, calls PythonCodeParser for Python files, and forwards parsed results to ProjectAnalyzer for relationship discovery.

Project-level analysis: maps modules, collects symbols, determines internal dependencies, discovers FastAPI router and include_router relations, resolves complete endpoint paths, and produces project statistics used for understanding and comparison.
Convert stored scan JSON into structured context for other steps: supports 'detailed' and 'llm' modes (detailed keeps full metadata, llm produces a compact context optimized for LLM prompts).

LLM orchestration for project understanding: builds LLM-ready context, constructs prompts (via app.services.llm.prompts), creates an LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory, calls provider.generate_json to get structured JSON understanding, and returns the parsed understanding for storage.
Factory for LLM providers: normalizes provider names and instantiates the concrete provider class (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) implementing BaseLLMProvider.

Abstract LLM provider interface: defines BaseLLMProvider with abstract generate_json and provider_name and a static helper parse_json_response that supports plain JSON, JSON inside markdown fences, and JSON embedded in surrounding text.
OpenAI-specific provider implementation using the Responses API: implements generate_json and provider_name.
Anthropic-specific provider implementation using the Messages API: implements generate_json and provider_name.

Compare two stored scans to detect added/removed/modified files, functions, classes, methods, routes, and internal dependencies; produces a comparison result used to decide whether to create an updated document.
Persist and retrieve scan JSON files under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json; supports save_scan, get_scan, get_latest_scan, and list_scans.

Persist and retrieve understanding JSON files under workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json; supports save_understanding, get_understanding, get_latest_understanding, and list_understandings.
Convert a stored understanding into an escaped, standalone HTML file (DocumentBuilder.build_html returns an HTML string).

Store generated HTML files and metadata. Provides save_document, list_documents, get_latest_document_metadata, get_document_metadata, and get_document_file methods to write and retrieve documents and metadata. Uses DOCUMENTS_PATH from app.core.config.

Streamlit UI helpers that normalize API URLs, build document URLs, call the documentation sync endpoint, and render results (status, identifiers, comparison summary, and document metadata) for user interaction.

api_overview.purpose: Health and basic root endpoint (app.get('/', tags=['Health'])).

Orchestrated documentation workflow: scans and saves the project, finds latest document, creates initial docs when none exist, compares scans when a document exists, creates a new document version if changes exist, and returns existing document when there are no changes. (response_model=DocumentationSyncResponse)
Scan, analyze, and save a local project (response_model=ProjectScanResponse).
Build compact LLM-ready project context from a stored scan (response_model=ProjectContextResponse).
Generate and save structured project understanding using the configured LLM (response_model=ProjectUnderstandingResponse).
Get the latest saved project understanding (response_model=StoredUnderstandingResponse).
Get the latest saved project scan (response_model=StoredScanResponse).
Generate and save HTML project documentation from a stored understanding (response_model=DocumentGenerationResponse, status_code=201).
Compare a new scan and create an updated documentation version if changes exist (response_model=DocumentUpdateResponse).
Get the latest generated project document metadata (response_model=DocumentSummaryResponse).
Serve the generated HTML documentation file for viewing or download (response_class=FileResponse).

key_dependencies.purpose: Used to scan a local project and produce a stored scan representation (imported name: ProjectScanner).
Used to persist and retrieve scan results (imported name: ScanStorage).
Used to generate an LLM understanding from a stored scan (imported name: ProjectUnderstandingService).
Used to persist and retrieve generated understandings (imported name: UnderstandingStorage).
Used to compare stored scans and produce change summaries driving document updates (imported name: ScanComparator).
Used to convert stored understandings into HTML documents (imported name: DocumentBuilder).
Used to save generated HTML documents and metadata (imported name: DocumentStorage).
ProjectScanner delegates Python file parsing to PythonCodeParser.parse_file (imported name: PythonCodeParser).
After parsing files, ProjectScanner delegates project-level analysis to ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project (imported name: ProjectAnalyzer).
Creates a concrete LLM provider (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) for generate_understanding (imported name: LLMProviderFactory).
Factory can instantiate OpenAIProvider when OpenAI is the selected provider (imported name: OpenAIProvider).
Factory can instantiate AnthropicProvider when Anthropic is the selected provider (imported name: AnthropicProvider).
DocumentStorage references DOCUMENTS_PATH from configuration for file storage location (imported name: DOCUMENTS_PATH).
UnderstandingStorage references UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH from configuration for file storage location (imported name: UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH).

risks_and_gaps.title: Synchronous/blocking behavior during LLM calls
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: There are no async functions reported in the codebase. Reasonable inference: LLM provider calls (OpenAIProvider.generate_json, AnthropicProvider.generate_json) and file IO operations appear synchronous and may block FastAPI worker threads while waiting for remote LLM responses or long file operations. This could limit concurrency and throughput under load.

risks_and_gaps.title: Error handling and retry behavior for LLM/provider failures
risks_and_gaps.description: Reasonable inference: The provided module list shows generate_json methods but no explicit mention of retries, exponential backoff, or rate limit handling. If provider API calls fail intermittently, lack of retries or standardized error translation could lead to failed document generation or unclear HTTP errors returned by endpoints.

risks_and_gaps.title: Authentication, authorization, and API security
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: No authentication or authorization modules are present in the scanned modules. Reasonable inference: API endpoints are exposed without built-in auth/ACL; this is a security gap for deployments exposing the service over a network.

risks_and_gaps.title: Configuration management and secret handling
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: app.core.config exposes get_llm_api_key and get_llm_provider. Reasonable inference: There is no scanned code showing secret encryption, secret rotation, or vault integration; secrets may be read from environment variables. The details of how load_dotenv is used and where environment values are stored are unclear from the context.

risks_and_gaps.title: Missing tests and CI indicators
risks_and_gaps.description: Reasonable inference: No tests or CI configuration files were listed in the provided context. Unit/integration tests for scanning correctness, comparator accuracy, and LLM prompt output parsing are not visible, indicating a testing/documentation gap.

risks_and_gaps.title: Storage consistency and concurrent writes
risks_and_gaps.description: Reasonable inference: Storage components save JSON files under workspace paths. The context does not show locking or transactional guarantees; concurrent requests that save scans/understandings/documents for the same project may race and cause inconsistent metadata.

risks_and_gaps.title: Prompts and model output validation reliability
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: BaseLLMProvider.parse_json_response supports different JSON wrapping cases. Reasonable inference: Robustness of parsing arbitrary model outputs into the exact structured understanding expected is a potential risk; malformed model output could break downstream document generation unless additional validation is enforced.

recommended_document_sections.title: Overview and Architecture
recommended_document_sections.purpose: High-level description of components (API, scanner, analyzer, context builder, LLM providers, storages, document builder) and how they interact; include a diagram and component responsibilities.

recommended_document_sections.title: Getting Started / Installation
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Step-by-step instructions to install dependencies, run install.bat or scripts/setup_runtime.ps1, and start the service using start.bat; include required Python version and platform notes.

recommended_document_sections.title: Configuration
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document environment variables, app.core.config functions (get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model), how load_dotenv is used, and where DOCUMENTS_PATH/UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH/workspace are configured.

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Complete listing of HTTP endpoints (paths, methods, request/response models from app.models.project), status codes, and example requests/responses for each endpoint.

recommended_document_sections.title: Runtime Flow and Examples
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Detailed examples showing typical flows: 1) full documentation sync (/documentation/sync), 2) standalone scan, 3) generating understanding, 4) generating/updating documents, including expected sequence diagrams and payload examples.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Providers and Prompting
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain LLM provider options, how LLMProviderFactory works, provider-specific configuration (OpenAI vs Anthropic), prompt templates (PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT and build_project_understanding_prompt), and guidance on model selection and rate limits.

recommended_document_sections.title: Storage and File Layout
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe workspace layout (workspace/scans, workspace/understandings, documents path), naming conventions for IDs and filenames, retention/cleanup recommendations, and how to retrieve stored artifacts.

recommended_document_sections.title: Error Handling and Operational Concerns
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document expected error codes (e.g., 404 mapping via _value_error_status), recommended retry/backoff strategies for LLM calls, logging recommendations, and how the API surfaces provider errors.

recommended_document_sections.title: Security and Deployment
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Recommendations for securing the API (TLS, authentication, rate-limiting), secrets management for LLM API keys, and deployment patterns (single-threaded vs multi-worker, asynchronous considerations).

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing and Validation
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Suggested test cases for parsers, analyzer, comparator, provider parsing (parse_json_response), and end-to-end integration tests; include sample fixtures and test scenarios for malformed LLM responses.

recommended_document_sections.title: Troubleshooting
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Common failure modes and remediation steps (e.g., parsing errors, model output parsing failures, file permission errors), plus diagnostic commands and log file locations.
